title: Enable WAL for concurrent reads
description: Switch to WAL mode so readers and a single writer proceed concurrently instead of blocking each other.
body: Use when more than one connection reads while another writes. Do not stay on the default rollback journal for a concurrent app, where a writer blocks all readers and vice versa. Actions: run PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL once (it persists in the database file); understand WAL lets many readers run during a write because the writer appends to a separate WAL file checkpointed later; keep the -wal and -shm sidecar files with the database. Validation signals include a test where readers return rows while a writer is mid-transaction. Risk boundary: WAL does not work over a network filesystem and still allows only one writer at a time; if writes are highly concurrent, that single-writer limit is the real ceiling to flag.
